sentences.translation: Người nào cũng có cái hay và cái dở của họ.

sentences.text: Masculine qualities in a woman are seen as strength, while feminine qualities in a man are viewed as weakness.

lessons.title: Artificial Intelligence — Example Sentences
lessons.description: Authentic example sentences for Artificial Intelligence, with Vietnamese translations.

sentences.translation: AI là Trí tuệ nhân tạo.
Trí tuệ nhân tạo làm mọi thứ dễ dàng hơn.
AI là Trí tuệ nhân tạo.

sentences.text: Will artificial intelligence make these jobs disappear?
sentences.translation: Liệu những công việc này có thể bị trí tuệ nhân tạo thay thế không?

sentences.text: Will artificial intelligence grow to kill humankind?
sentences.translation: Liệu trí tuệ nhân tạo sau này có huỷ diệt nhân loại không?

sentences.text: There's no such thing as Artificial Intelligence.
sentences.translation: Không có cái gọi là trí tuệ nhân tạo.

sentences.text: Will these jobs be replaced by artificial intelligence?
sentences.translation: Liệu những công việc này có thể bị trí tuệ nhân tạo thay thế không?

sentences.text: This isn't Leon's actual voice. It's artificial intelligence.
sentences.translation: Đó không phải giọng thật của Leon. Nó là trí tuệ nhân tạo.

sentences.text: Maybe someday, artificial intelligence will create new languages for humans to use.
sentences.translation: Có lẽ một ngày nào đó, trí tuệ nhân tạo sẽ tạo ra những ngôn ngữ mới để con người sử dụng.

sentences.text: Benedito's work has the potential to advance the field of artificial intelligence.
sentences.translation: Công trình của Benedito có tiềm năng thúc đẩy lĩnh vực trí tuệ nhân tạo.
Tôi không phải là robot.
Robot có thể hoạt động trong điều kiện nguy hiểm.

vocab.meaningEn: Niceness.
A sports competition, especially for track and field (a track meet) or swimming (a swim meet).
An expression of appreciation; a thought.
exampleSentences: "How do you do, Mr James?" "Fine, thanks for asking. Nice to meet you."

vocab.meaningEn: To request (information, or an answer to a question).
exampleSentences: "How do you do, Mr James?" "Fine, thanks for asking. Nice to meet you."

vocab.meaningEn: Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
exampleSentences: It's rare to meet nice people like you.

vocab.meaningEn: A daughter of the same parents as another person; a female sibling.

exampleSentences: My family goes skiing every winter.

vocab.meaningEn: Coherent; well organized.
exampleSentences: Every evening, my family has dinner together.

vocab.meaningEn: At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
exampleSentences: She gets up the earliest in my family.

vocab.meaningEn: One who officially belongs to a group.
The time of the day between dusk and night, when it gets dark.
exampleSentences: Every evening, my family has dinner together.

vocab.meaningEn: To make somebody busy or active; to occupy.
exampleSentences: Mother is the busiest in my family.

vocab.meaningEn: An act of thinking; consideration (of something).
To move on skis
exampleSentences: My family goes skiing every winter.

vocab.meaningEn: Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
exampleSentences: My family goes skiing every winter.

vocab.meaningEn: The part of the day from dawn to noon.
To move on the feet by alternately setting each foot (or pair or group of feet, in the case of animals with four or more feet) forward, with at least one foot on the ground at all times. Compare run.
exampleSentences: Tom walks his dog every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: (heading) Employment.
exampleSentences: I don't wanna go to work.

vocab.meaningEn: The first meal of the day, usually eaten in the morning.
To set free from restraint or danger.
exampleSentences: The postman delivers letters every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: A person who studies or learns about a particular subject.
exampleSentences: The students go to school every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: Someone (implied male) who delivers the post (mail) to, and/or collects the post from, residential or commercial addresses, or from public mailboxes.
exampleSentences: The postman delivers letters every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: A symbol in an alphabet.
exampleSentences: The postman delivers letters every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: A stopping place.
exampleSentences: Amy walks to the station every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: To make something accessible or allow for passage by moving from a shut position.
exampleSentences: The door is opened every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: (collective) A group of fish or a group of marine mammals such as porpoises, dolphins, or whales.
exampleSentences: The students go to school every morning.

vocab.meaningEn: A multitude; a great aggregate; a mass of people; the generality; the common herd.
To be in the habit of choosing something rather than something else; to favor; to like better.
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
(official BIPM spelling, Australian, New Zealand, Irish, Indian and South African spelling) An SI unit of length equal to 103 metres. Symbol: km
exampleSentences: How many kilometers did you run?

vocab.meaningEn: In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
exampleSentences: How many kilograms did you lose?

vocab.meaningEn: A person who has not yet reached adulthood, whether natural (puberty), cultural (initiation), or legal (majority)
A symbol in an alphabet.
exampleSentences: How many letters do you have?

vocab.meaningEn: Official designation for currency in some parts of the world, including Canada, the United States, Australia, New Zealand, Hong Kong, and elsewhere. Its symbol is $.
exampleSentences: How many yen is four dollars?

vocab.meaningEn: Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
